key_insight: A D-gun sprayed Al2O3-TiO2 coating significantly improves the tribological performance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest best predicts wear from sliding conditions.
engineering_relevance: This work supports using ceramic thermal-spray coatings plus data-driven wear prediction to extend the service life of aerospace, biomedical, and marine titanium components.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating on Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spraying.
Tested wear with pin-on-disc (ASTM G99) under 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Trained ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from process/load parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM showed reduced wear and a protective tribo-layer on coated samples, and random forest achieved the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error among the models.
watch_item: The machine-learning models appear to be based on a limited experimental parameter range, so generalization beyond the tested loads, speeds, and coating conditions should be validated.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
